highlights.text: [[reply_to_current]]Reset complete—`agent:main:main` was wiped from the session store, the old transcript/lock files were removed, and the fresh session you’re in now is running on **GPT‑4.1‑mini** with an empty context window. --- ### How to add OpenRouter models (e.g., Grok-2) 1. **Get an API key** - Create one at <https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys>. 2. **Register the key with OpenClaw** ```bash export OPENROUTER_API_KEY="sk-or-..." openclaw onboard --auth-choice apiKey \ --token-provider openrouter \ --token "$OPENROUTER_API_KEY" ``` (You can also drop `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` into `~/.openclaw/.env` so the launch agent gets it.) 3. **Add the model(s) to config** ```json5 { agents: { defaults: { models: { "openrouter/moonshotai/grok-2": { alias: "grok2" }, }, model: { primary: "openai/gpt-4.1-mini", fallbacks: [ "openrouter/moonshotai/grok-2", "openai/gpt-5.1-codex", "openai-codex/gpt-5.4", "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6", ], }, }, }, } ``` Apply it with `openclaw config patch`, then run `openclaw models list` to confirm you see the OpenRouter entries. 4. **Use it on demand** - In chat: `/model grok2` (or the full ID) to force that model for the current turn. - In tools/scripts: reference `openrouter/<provider>/<model>`. --- ### How to add Google Gemini (Flash or Pro) 1. **Create a Gemini API key** - Go to <https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey> and copy the key. 2. **Register it** ```bash export GEMINI_API_KEY="..." openclaw onboard --auth-choice google-api-key \ --gemini-api-key "$GEMINI_API_KEY" ``` 3. **Add desired models to config** ```json5 { agents: { defaults: { models: { "google/gemini-2.0-flash": { alias: "flash" }, "google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview": { alias: "g3pro" }, }, model: { primary: "openai/gpt-4.1-mini", fallbacks: [ "google/gemini-2.0-flash", "openrouter/moonshotai/grok-2", "openai/gpt-5.1-codex", "openai-codex/gpt-5.4", "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6", ], }, }, }, } ``` Again, apply via `openclaw config patch` and verify with `openclaw models list`. 4. **Optional extras** - For Gemini’s media features, make sure the gateway service can see `GEMINI_API_KEY` (either in the LaunchAgent plist or `~/.openclaw/.env`). - If you need OAuth instead of API keys, use the `google-gemini-cli` provider as documented in `/providers/google.md`. --- Let me know when you’re ready—I can add the OpenRouter + Gemini entries for you once you have the keys, and we can decide exactly where they sit in the fallback order.
